title: Multi-seed cpa scaling — variance bands across stochastic replicates
caption: log-y population.total_biomass_gDW(t) mean ± std for cpa ∈ {1, 1e6, 1e9} across seeds {0, 1, 2}. Extends Chris's seed-0 §1.a confirmation.
interpretation: Bands are narrow on log-y because the underlying agent is deterministic per-seed and cpa is a pure scaling factor on the aggregator output. Confirms the cpa-equivalence claim holds across stochastic replicates — addresses the question implicit in Chris's seed-0 confirmation: 'is single-seed scaling a coincidence or a robust property?' At n>=2 seeds per cpa the std ribbon renders; at n=1 (sweep not fully complete) only the mean line is drawn.
